content.full_text:
MWD
METROPOLITAN WATER DISTRICT OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA

Office of General Counsel

Telephone: (213) 250-6308
Telecopier: (213) 250-6890

September 14, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Counsel, Legal Department
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attention Blanca Breeze

Proper Financial Disclosure Methods

Dear Ms. Breeze:

With reference to our telephone conversation of this
date, you will recall that I expressed concern over the
question of proper financial disclosure methods for directors
and top staff of The Metropolitan Water District of Southern
California as a result of the amendment of Government Code
section 87200, in particular the addition of the term "other
public officials who manage public investments." Presently the
subject officials are designated employees under the District's
Conflict of Interest and Disclosure of Personal Finances Code
and as such, file annual disclosure statements using form 730.
If section 87200 is found to be applicable to these
individuals, they would be deleted from the District's list of
designated employees and would file form 721 directly with the
Commission.

Based upon our discussion, it is my understanding
that the regulations interpreting the scope of section 87200 as
amended will not be finalized until December 1990 at the
earliest and possibly not then. Accordingly, the District
should not at this time initiate amendments to our Code to
delete the directors and top staff from the list of designated
employees who are required to file form 730, pending the
adoption of regulations implementing the amendments to section
87200. It is also my understanding that since the period for
annual filing will commence January 1, 1991, for calendar year
1990, we should assume that the subject officials will file for
1990 using form 730 rather than form 721, and will continue to
do so until final regulations relating to the amendments to
section 87200 are adopted and can be implemented.

1111 Sunset Boulevard, Los Angeles, California • Mailing address: Box 54153, Los Angeles, California 90054

The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California

-2-

I would appreciate your confirmation that my
understanding of these matters as discussed above is correct.
Sincerely,

Fred Vendig
General Counsel

James W. Mountain
Deputy General Counsel

JWM:db
(jb:FPPC)

Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Attention Blanca Breeze Proper Financial Disclosure Methods Dear Ms. Breeze: With reference to our telephone conversation of this date, you will recall that I expressed concern over the question of proper financial disclosure methods for directors and top staff of The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California as a result of the amendment of Government Code section 87200, in particular the addition of the term "other public officials who manage public investments." Presently the subject officials are designated employees under the District's Conflict of Interest and Disclosure of Personal Finances Code and as such, file annual disclosure statements using form 730. If section 87200 is found to be applicable to these individuals, they would be deleted from the District's list of designated employees and would file form 721 directly with the Commission. Based upon our discussion, it is my understanding that the regulations interpreting the scope of section 87200 as amended will not be finalized until December 1990 at the earliest and possibly not then. Accordingly, the District should not at this time initiate amendments to our Code to delete the directors and top staff from the list of designated employees who are required to file form 730, pending the adoption of regulations implementing the amendments to section 87200. It is also my understanding that since the period for annual filing will commence January 1, 1991, for calendar year 1990, we should assume that the subject officials will file for 1990 using form 730 rather than form 721, and will continue to do so until final regulations relating to the amendments to section 87200 are adopted and can be implemented. 1111 Sunset Boulevard, Los Angeles, California • Mailing address: Box 54153, Los Angeles, California 90054 The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California -2- I would appreciate your confirmation that my understanding of these matters as discussed above is correct. Sincerely, Fred Vendig General Counsel James W. Mountain Deputy General Counsel JWM:db (jb:FPPC)
